mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending power and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that compresses corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered periods, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets price in, causing a repricing of equity risk premiums and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations where CPIAUCSL accelerates alongside rising FEDFUNDS, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and fixed investment flows directly increase corporate revenue visibility, leading to positive earnings revisions that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant difference between high and low RSAFS growth terciles (p > 0.05), or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105 on the 2017=100 index, manufacturing capacity utilization peaks, signaling robust corporate output and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 105, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% in a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rapid employment growth and rising federal funds rates indicate an overheating economy where aggressive monetary tightening will inevitably contract demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-FEDFUNDS signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of co-occurring high-value periods, or the interaction term's marginal effect becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, quarterly GDP contraction combined with elevated unemployment triggers a recessionary earnings shock, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations where GDPC1 contracts alongside rising UNRATE, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below chance over a 3-year out-of-sample period.
